name: Sourdough Starter and Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the skim milk in a glass container and allow it to stand at room temperature for 24 hours.
2. Stir in the bread flour and yeast.
3. Leave the mixture uncovered in a warm place for 2-5 days, or until it bubbles and sour.
4. If it starts to dry out, stir in enough tepid water to bring it back to the original consistency.
5. Once it has a good sour aroma and is full of bubbles, it is ready to use. Maintain about 1 and 1/2 cups of starter.
6. Each time part of the starter is used, replenish with an equal amount of milk and flour.
7. Leave at room temperature several hours or overnight, then cover and store in the refrigerator.
8. Use the starter at least once a week. If not used for 2-3 weeks, discard about half and replenish as described.
Sourdough Bread:
1. In a bowl, combine 2/3 cup skim milk, sourdough starter, bread flour, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tsp lite salt, and 1 1/2 tsp yeast.
2. Mix thoroughly using half of the flour.
3. Let stand uncovered to ferment overnight in a warm place.
4. The next morning, after the mixture has risen and fallen, stir down any crust that may have formed.
5. Add the remaining flour. When thoroughly mixed, turn out onto a board covered with a little flour to prevent sticking.
6. Shape dough into 2 loaves, place into bread pans, brush lightly with liquid Butter Buds, and let rise covered until almost doubled in size.
7. Bake in a pre-heated oven at 400°F for 45-50 minutes.
Note: This recipe works GREAT in a bread machine. Simply put all of the ingredients in the machine, adding the dry ingredients first. Yields: 2 loaves (24 slices)
Per slice Fat: 0.21g Calories: 96 Cholesterol:0.42 mg Carbohydrate:19.7g Protein:2.89g Sodium:10.3mg
